notes: Integrin alpha-6; reviewed Swiss-Prot, 1130 aa. Direct binding partner of P. stomatis FBA on colonocyte surface; bridges bacterial adhesin to ERBB2 activation (Huang et al. 2024).
Integrin beta-4; reviewed Swiss-Prot, 1822 aa. Direct binding partner of P. stomatis FBA on colonocyte surface; bridges bacterial adhesin to ERBB2 activation (Huang et al. 2024).

WP_029950202.1 (locus tag T262_RS0106475 in P. micra strain A293) is the reference strain ortholog of Gene_1539 in P. micra strain Pm-42, which was experimentally characterized by Chang et al. 2026 as an ADP-ribosyltransferase (SirTM) that modifies histone H2B in human colonocytes and drives colorectal cancer progression via the CEBPβ/SAA1/IL-17C pathway. Gene identity confirmed by exact match of forward qPCR primer 1539-F within the CDS and 20/22 match of reverse primer 1539-R within the CDS (primers from Chang et al. Supplementary Table S1). The protein is 97.9-100% conserved at the amino acid level across assembled P. micra strains (BLAST vs nr). Note: NCBI auto-annotation labels this protein as a SIR2-family deacylase based on domain prediction, but Chang et al. demonstrated experimentally that it lacks deacetylase activity and functions solely as an ADP-ribosyltransferase (Chang et al. 2026, Figure 5C).

Fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase class II (gene fba); 307 aa; unreviewed TrEMBL. Surface-displayed moonlighting glycolytic enzyme that acts as the sole characterized virulence factor of P. stomatis. Binds human integrins ITGA6/ITGB4 on colonocyte surface, activating ERBB2-MEK-ERK-p90RSK signaling and driving CRC progression and RTK-inhibitor resistance. Strain: DSM 17678 (HMP draft genome, BioProject PRJNA34073). Full cascade: FBA → ITGA6/ITGB4 → ERBB2 → MEK → ERK → p90RSK (Huang et al. 2024, Cell Host Microbe, DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2024.07.001). No LPS enzyme or dedicated adhesin effectors are characterized for P. stomatis in the current literature; FBA is the sole validated virulence factor.
